title: Dark Gooey Argentinian Beef Empanadas
description: A citrusy and gooey Argentinian dish featuring salted butter and score fat, perfect for a midnight craving, this flexitarian friendly empanada is a must try. It combines the richness of beef with the brightness of citrus.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large mixing bowl, combine flour and melted salted butter to make the empanada dough.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and roll each into a ball.
Roll out each ball of dough into a thin circle.
In a separate pan, cook the ground beef over medium heat until browned, breaking it into small pieces as it cooks.
Add diced onion and minced garlic to the pan and cook until the onion is translucent.
Stir in score fat and orange zest into the beef mixture.
Place a tablespoon of the beef mixture onto the center of each dough circle.
Fold the dough in half over the filling and press the edges together to seal the empanada.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with egg wash.
Bake the empanadas in the preheated oven for 20 minutes, or until golden brown.
Cook ground beef to an internal temperature of 71°C / 160°F (no longer pink in the middle) before serving.
